title: 25 and 26
wikitext:
[]: 25' and 26'             Quote Post by Houndour » Sat Feb 03, 2007 10:29 pm As those of you with the Platinum Thinpak know, only the NPC versions of 21-24 are included, which is mildly annoying to say the least. I believe the original Platinum release of the series included all NPC episodes, but I'm not prepared to spend however much more just for two of them. Anywho, the point of this thread - has ADV released these episodes apart from the rest of the series? Furthermore, is the added animation in them just snagged from Death? That was the case for the majority of 21-24', right?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Feb 03, 2007 10:38 pm 21'-24' ARE the only NPC episodes. Genesis Reborn, the DVD that contains 24' and the Video/LD release of 25 and 26, doesn't have anything new from the perfect collection's 25 and 26, which was the On-Air versions.The version of Death that Manga released is Death True^2 (I'm pretty sure) which had some of the extra NPC footage removed to promote sales of the Ressurrection (ep21'-23') and Genesis Reborn DVDs. The platinum release contains the remastered versions of 25 and 26.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Feb 03, 2007 10:59 pm What Ornette said in simple terms is, there is no NPC of 25 and 26. 25' and 26' are also known as EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Houndour » Sat Feb 03, 2007 11:01 pm Ornette wrote: 21'-24' ARE the only NPC episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Feb 05, 2007 3:34 pm 21-24: On-air episodes 21'-24'-Footage from the orginal Death, removed from Death(true)(?) and Death(true)^2 while added into 21-24, thereby making them Director's Cuts/New Production Cuts. Onto EoTV and EoE: 25: The World Ending/Do You Love Me? 26: The Beast that shouted I("Ai") at the heart of the world/Finale: Take Care of Yourself aka the final two episodes, "EoTV" End of TV. 25': AIR/Love is Destructive 26': My Pure Heart for You/One More Final: I Need You aka The End of Evangelion Make sense now?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Mon Feb 05, 2007 4:43 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: 21-24: On-air episodes 21'-24'-Footage from the orginal Death, removed from Death(true)(?) and Death(true)^2 while added into 21-24, thereby making them Director's Cuts/New Production Cuts.

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Mon Feb 05, 2007 4:52 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: 21-24: On-air episodes 21'-24'-Footage from the orginal Death, removed from Death(true)(?) and Death(true)^2 while added into 21-24, thereby making them Director's Cuts/New Production Cuts. Make sense now?

[]:             Quote Post by Houndour » Mon Feb 05, 2007 6:17 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Make sense now?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Feb 05, 2007 6:50 pm Shin-seiki wrote: Sailor Star Dust wrote: 21-24: On-air episodes 21'-24'-Footage from the orginal Death, removed from Death(true)(?) and Death(true)^2 while added into 21-24, thereby making them Director's Cuts/New Production Cuts.

I believe the original Platinum release of the series included all NPC episodes, but I'm not prepared to spend however much more just for two of them. Anywho, the point of this thread - has ADV released these episodes apart from the rest of the series? Furthermore, is the added animation in them just snagged from Death? That was the case for the majority of 21-24', right?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Feb 03, 2007 10:38 pm 21'-24' ARE the only NPC episodes. Genesis Reborn, the DVD that contains 24' and the Video/LD release of 25 and 26, doesn't have anything new from the perfect collection's 25 and 26, which was the On-Air versions.The version of Death that Manga released is Death True^2 (I'm pretty sure) which had some of the extra NPC footage removed to promote sales of the Ressurrection (ep21'-23') and Genesis Reborn DVDs. The platinum release contains the remastered versions of 25 and 26.
